items.summary: MORE than 160,000 bitter fans have signed a petition demanding an investigation into Scotland's win over Haiti. items.title: Spurs agree £52m Van Hecke deal with Brighton
items.summary: Netherlands World Cup defender Jan Paul van Hecke is set to leave Brighton for Tottenham Hotspur after the two clubs agree a £52m deal.

items.title: Thomas Partey loses appeal after being denied entry into Canada as ex-Arsenal midfielder set to miss opening match
items.summary: THOMAS PARTEY has been ruled out of Ghana’s World Cup opener after losing his appeal against a travel ban. items.title: England's Livramento out of WCup, Chalobah in
items.summary: Tino Livramento has been ruled out of the World Cup with a hamstring injury and will be replaced by Chelsea defender Trevoh Chalobah, England announced on Tuesday.

items.title: Ghana appeal Canada's denial of Partey WC visa
items.summary: Ghana officials on Tuesday appealed Canada's decision to refuse midfielder Thomas Partey entry into the country for the team's first World Cup match while he awaits trial on rape charges.

items.title: 'Repressed' Iran forced to leave U.S. after game
items.summary: Iran coach Amir Ghalenoei called his team perhaps the "most oppressed team in the whole World Cup," after he said Iran have been forced to leave Los Angeles and return to their base camp in Tijuana, Mexico, immediately following their opening match.
